articles.title: Improper Joinder Of Causes Of Action Not Grounds To Reject Plaint Under Order VII Rule 11: Bombay High Court
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has held that an objection regarding improper joinder of causes of action raises a triable issue and cannot be a ground for rejection of a plaint under Order VII Rule 11 of the Code of Civil Procedure. The Court observed that such objections require adjudication on evidence and cannot justify dismissal of a suit at the threshold. Justice Gauri Godse was hearing an interim application filed by Victoria Enterprises Limited seeking rejection of the plaint in a commercial suit

articles.title: Cabinet nod to Rs 1,718 cr MSP funding to Cotton Corp

This ensures farmers receive fair returns and prevents distress sales.

articles.title: India's privatisation drive derails because of weak investor interest
articles.summary: India faces challenges in its privatisation program. Three planned sales are under consideration for shelving due to weak investor interest. This follows the failed sale of IDBI Bank. Dwindling buyer interest, high valuations, and geopolitical uncertainty are impacting the divestment drive. The government's ambitious plan to exit most sectors is encountering fresh setbacks.
articles.raw_summary: India faces challenges in its privatisation program. Three planned sales are under consideration for shelving due to weak investor interest. This follows the failed sale of IDBI Bank. Dwindling buyer interest, high valuations, and geopolitical uncertainty are impacting the divestment drive. The government's ambitious plan to exit most sectors is encountering fresh setbacks.

articles.title: Indian Bank, NaBFID to raise Rs 8 crore via bonds by March
articles.summary: Indian Bank and National ​Bank for Financing Infrastructure ​and Development plan to raise a combined 80 ​billion rupees ($864.10 million) from the corporate bond market by March, two bankers with knowledge of the matter said on Wednesday.

articles.title: Oil woes, capital outflows drag rupee to record low
articles.summary: The Indian ​rupee fell to a record low on Wednesday, ​extending a rough patch as the raging conflict in the Middle East kept oil prices elevated, raising macroeconomic risks for Asia's third-largest economy and sparking ‌capital outflows.

articles.title: Rupee hits historic low, slips past 92.62 vs USD as Middle East tensions keep energy worries in focus
articles.summary: The Indian rupee hit a new lifetime low of 92.62 against the dollar on Monday, extending recent losses. Elevated oil prices, driven by the Middle East conflict, are increasing economic risks for India and deterring capital inflows. Brent crude has surged approximately 40% since the conflict began, impacting global energy markets.

Brent crude has surged approximately 40% since the conflict began, impacting global energy markets.

articles.title: Axis Bank shares rise 2% as lender set to invest Rs 1,500 crore into NBFC arm Axis Finance
articles.summary: Axis Bank shares gained after the lender approved a Rs 1,500 crore capital infusion into its NBFC arm, Axis Finance. The move follows a pause in stake sale plans amid regulatory changes. The investment aims to support growth, while the subsidiary prepares to present a revised strategy and capital requirements.

articles.title: US Fed outcome today amid Iran war: What to expect and what it means for Indian markets
articles.summary: The US Federal Reserve is expected to hold rates amid rising uncertainty from the Iran conflict and mixed economic signals. Markets will closely track the Fed’s commentary for cues on rate cuts. Indian equities may react to global liquidity trends, crude oil movement and potential FII flows influenced by US policy signals.

articles.title: Delhivery announces economy air parcel service to UK, Canada, Australia
articles.summary: Logistics firm Delhivery is expanding its international air parcel service. The service now reaches the UK, Canada, and Australia. Delhivery's platform offers simplified shipping and customs documentation for these markets.

articles.title: QSR chain Burger Singh raises $8.8 million led by Artal Asia
articles.summary: Burger Singh raised $8.8 million (Rs 82 crore) led by Artal Asia, reaching a Rs 520 crore valuation. It runs over 200 stores across 100 cities.

articles.title: ‘Why have you stopped paying?' Punjab to seek water royalty from Rajasthan; Mann claims Rs 1.44 lakh crore dues since 1960
articles.summary: Punjab's Chief Minister Bhagwant Mann has demanded royalty from Rajasthan for water supplied since 1960, citing a 1920 tripartite agreement. Mann stated Punjab has formally written to Rajasthan and plans to involve the central government to resolve the unpaid dues.

Despite challenges, Iran's healthcare system remains largely functional, with contingency plans in place for worsening conditions and potential health risks from environmental factors.

articles.title: India opens anti-dumping probe into ethyl chloroformate imports from China
articles.summary: India has launched an anti-dumping probe into Chinese ethyl chloroformate imports following a complaint by domestic producer Paushak, alleging unfair pricing that harms local industry. The investigation, initiated by the Directorate General of Trade Remedies, will assess if duties are needed to protect Indian manufacturers. This move is part of a broader effort to curb cheap imports.

articles.summary: Escalating Middle East tensions are impacting Indian households and businesses reliant on LPG imports. To counter supply disruptions, the government is urging a swift shift to Piped Natural Gas (PNG), fast-tracking approvals and waiving charges for pipeline infrastructure. This initiative aims to ease the strain on LPG supplies, with hospitality and industrial sectors already facing significant challenges.

Reforms are suggested for fiscal prudence and transparency in off-budget borrowings.

articles.title: RBI seeks banks’ views on AI-based facial recognition tools at ATMs, branches to curb fraud
articles.summary: As per the draft guidelines, a bona fide victim, being an individual person and having lodged a complaint involving gross loss of an amount up to Rs 50,000 on account of fraudulent electronic banking transaction (s), would be compensated 85% of the net loss amount (calculated after reducing recoveries made, whether before or after paying the compensation, from the gross loss amount), or Rs 25,000, whichever is less, once during his or her lifetime.

articles.title: LPG and oil crisis updates - March 13: LPG imports a slight concern, official says; consumers urged to consider switching to piped gas
articles.summary: The Oil Ministry reports a 30% increase in domestic LPG production since March 5 and assures uninterrupted household supply. Officials express slight concern over LPG imports and urge consumers to consider switching to piped gas. The government's actions and recommendations reflect policy-level interventions in the energy sector.

articles.title: Support measures for exporters on anvil to deal with West Asia crisis
articles.summary: The commerce ministry is set to announce support measures for Indian exporters facing disruptions due to the West Asia crisis. These measures, potentially including insurance assistance, aim to mitigate the impact of increased freight costs and premiums. The government is actively monitoring the situation and collaborating with various departments to aid the trading community.

articles.title: Consent: The Foundation Of Digital Data Protection
articles.summary: Consensus facit legem, which means consent makes the law. Primacy of consent is not only confined to the formation of contracts but also underpins a wide array of legal relationships and highlights a philosophical commitment to individual autonomy. articles.title: Article 25 Protects Right To Congregate For Worship But Forbids Incitement; No Embargo On Prayers In Private Premises: Allahabad HC
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court on Monday held that Article 25 of the Constitution of India protects the right to congregate for worship for every religious denomination in the country, but it does not accord protection to incitement of one faith by another in the garb of prayer. articles.title: Legal Proceedings Led To Martyrdom Of Bhagat Singh, Sukhdev And Rajguru
articles.summary: Bhagat Singh faced two significant trials during his lifetime. The first arose from the Central Legislative Assembly Bomb incident in Delhi, while the second was the Lahore Conspiracy Case concerning the killing of Mr. Saunders in retaliation for the death of Lala Lajpat Rai. articles.title: NDPS Act | Prosecution Must Prove Actual Cultivation Of Cannabis, Mere Presence On Land Not Enough: Bombay High Court
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court has held that in cases involving the alleged cultivation of cannabis under the NDPS Act, the burden lies on the prosecution to establish through cogent evidence that the accused was actually cultivating the plants, and not merely present at the spot or named in land records. The Court observed that asking the accused to prove that he was not cultivating the land would amount to imposing an impermissible negative burden contrary to settled principles of criminal jurisprudenc
articles.raw_summary: The Bombay High Court has held that in cases involving the alleged cultivation of cannabis under the NDPS Act, the burden lies on the prosecution to establish through cogent evidence that the accused was actually cultivating the plants, and not merely present at the spot or named in land records. articles.title: Writ Petition Dismissed as DRT Order Appealable Under SARFAESI Act: AP HC
articles.summary: The High Court held that DRT orders are appealable under Section 18 of the SARFAESI Act. It ruled that writ jurisdiction cannot be invoked without exhausting the statutory remedy. articles.title: Exemption Allowed as Corpus Donations Excluded from Annual Receipts U/s. 10(23C)(iiiad)
articles.summary: The Tribunal held that corpus donations received with specific directions are capital in nature and cannot be included in annual receipts. Since actual receipts were below ₹1 crore, exemption was allowed. The post Exemption Allowed as Corpus Donations Excluded from Annual Receipts U/s. 10(23C)(iiiad) appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Offense of money laundering is distinct and different from predicate offense
articles.summary: Madras High Court held that money laundering offense is independent from predicate offense. Thus, conviction under Prevention of Corruption Act and subsequent Prevention of Money Laundering Act prosecution doesn’t constitute double jeopardy under Article 20(2) of the Constitution of India. The post Offense of money laundering is distinct and different from predicate offense appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Reassessment u/s. 148 after four years untenable as full and true material facts disclosed
articles.summary: ITAT Chennai held that reassessment u/s. 148 of the Income Tax Act after expiry of four years not sustainable since there was no failure on the part of assessee to disclose fully and truly all material facts. Further, reassessment is invalid for non-furnishing of actual reasons recorded. The post Reassessment u/s. 148 after four years untenable as full and true material facts disclosed appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Kolkata quashed Reassessment as Time-Barred Due to Expiry of Limitation Period
articles.summary: The Tribunal examined whether reassessment proceedings were valid when initiated beyond the statutory time limit. It held that the notice issued under Section 148 was barred by limitation and invalid. The ruling emphasizes strict adherence to limitation provisions in reassessment cases. The U.S. faces limited options in the region, which could affect global energy supplies and trade. For India, this situation is relevant because it relies heavily on oil imports through this strait, making it a potential policy concern.
articles.raw_summary: In the strait, Iran holds the advantage—and America has no good options.

This trend may prompt policy responses from the government or regulators, given its impact on fuel costs and broader economic stability.

articles.summary: Punjab’s Economic Survey 2025-26 identifies economic stagnation and a mismatch between job opportunities and workforce skills as key reasons for the state’s brain drain. Experts note a combination of 'push' factors (lack of local opportunities) and 'pull' factors (demand elsewhere) driving migration. The survey underscores the need for policy interventions to address these challenges.
articles.raw_summary: Punjab’s Economic Survey 2025-26 notes that a combination of “pull” and “push” factors behind brain drain from the State.

articles.summary: A parliamentary panel has recommended expanding co-lending partnerships between banks and non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) to help micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) access cheaper credit. The committee also proposed listing profitable regional rural banks and strengthening state-run general insurers. It highlighted a credit gap for small businesses and declining insurance penetration, suggesting measures like climate risk insurance and using business correspondents to reach rur
articles.raw_summary: A parliamentary panel suggests boosting bank and NBFC co-lending to help MSMEs access cheaper credit. It also proposes listing profitable regional rural banks and reinforcing state-run general insurers. The committee highlighted a significant credit gap for small businesses and a decline in insurance penetration. Recommendations include developing climate risk insurance and using business correspondents to reach rural areas.

articles.title: Easier FDI norms notified for border country investors
articles.summary: India has updated its foreign direct investment policy. Investments from countries sharing a land border now face stricter government scrutiny. Overseas companies with up to 10% Chinese shareholding can invest via the automatic route, subject to limits. However, entities registered in China or other bordering nations require government approval. This change aims to regulate investments from neighboring countries.

articles.title: Govt imposes import curbs on silver jewellery studded with diamonds

The policy aims to regulate imports of such jewellery.

A prolonged supply crunch will drive up food bills.

The initiative aims to boost PNG adoption as a cleaner and more reliable alternative to LPG.

articles.title: NCERT textbook row: Priyanka Chaturvedi flags ‘judicial dictatorship’ in RS after SC bar on authors

The court's order has sparked debate over judicial overreach and its impact on educational content and academic freedom.

articles.title: Supreme Court says paid menstrual leave will end women’s careers, calls PIL a bid to ‘instil fear’
articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a Public Interest Litigation (PIL) seeking paid menstrual leave, stating that such policies could negatively impact women's careers. The court said the PIL aimed to 'instil fear' by suggesting women are not on par with men during menstruation. The ruling has implications for workplace policies and gender equality in India.
articles.raw_summary: Dismissing PIL, SC said: 'This is only to create impression in young women that you have some natural issues, and are not at par with males, and you cannot work like them during a particular time.'

articles.title: SC sets record straight for OBC quota—social status, not paycheck must be measure for ‘creamy layer’
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that social status, not just parental income, should determine 'creamy layer' status for OBC reservations. This decision impacts students who were denied benefits based solely on their parents' salaries. The ruling clarifies eligibility for government-backed educational opportunities under affirmative action policies.
articles.raw_summary: Students involved in legal challenges were successful Civil Services Examination candidates whose OBC (non-creamy layer) status was rejected based on parents’ salaries.

The ruling, based on Harish Rana's case, underscores that abandonment is a greater tragedy than death, setting a precedent for medical and legal practices in India.
articles.raw_summary: Harish Rana’s case revealed a fundamental truth about human existence: ‘The greatest tragedy in life is not death, but abandonment’, the top court said in its landmark judgement.

articles.title: Nazara Technologies to acquire 50% stake in Spain-based Bluetile, BestPlay for $100 million
articles.summary: Gaming firm Nazara Technologies will acquire a 50% controlling stake in Spain’s Bluetile Games and BestPlay Systems for $100.3 million (Rs 918 crore), its largest deal yet. The move will be done via Nazara Technologies UK. The transaction also includes performance-linked earnouts of up to $98.2 million, contingent on achieving revenue and profitability targets between 2027 and 2029.

articles.summary: B2B ecommerce firm Udaan plans to shift its headquarters from Singapore to India as part of a reverse merger, aiming for an IPO within 9–18 months. articles.title: India oilmeal exports hit by US-Iran conflict, Red Sea disruptions
articles.summary: Escalating US-Iran tensions are disrupting India's oilmeal exports, with significant portions to the Middle East and Europe facing logistics challenges due to Red Sea and Strait of Hormuz instability. articles.title: India growth outlook dims as oil tops $100, gas crisis deepens
articles.summary: A distant war is impacting India's economy. Soaring oil prices and gas shortages are affecting homes and businesses. This energy crisis threatens economic growth and raises inflation concerns. Industries are halting operations. The situation is creating strain for everyday citizens and impacting employment. The external sector faces significant risks as well.

articles.title: What's key to India's forex defence? Patra suggests it's $1 trillion buffer
articles.summary: Former RBI Deputy Governor Michael Patra advocates for India to build foreign exchange reserves to at least $1 trillion. This substantial buffer is crucial for market intervention, covering external debt obligations and cushioning against portfolio investment outflows. Recent sharp declines highlight the pressure on reserves amid global uncertainties.

articles.summary: Indian exporters are facing significant challenges due to global shipping route disruptions. The Bharat Chamber of Commerce has requested the Reserve Bank of India to provide crucial banking support. This includes enhanced working capital, flexible credit, and extended repayment periods. The aim is to safeguard exporters from financial strain and ensure continued trade flow amidst these global uncertainties.

articles.summary: The WTO landscape is heating up as India encounters objections from the US, Australia, Paraguay, and Ukraine regarding its agricultural support practices. articles.title: India’s wholesale inflation rises to 11-month high of 2.13% in February
articles.summary: India's wholesale price inflation reached an 11-month high of 2.13% in February, driven by increased costs of primary goods like food and non-food articles. Economists anticipate further acceleration due to rising input costs and ongoing geopolitical tensions, with manufacturing products also showing a slight increase.

articles.title: Trade deficit nearly 2x in Feb; exports face battlefield ahead
articles.summary: India's trade deficit widened significantly in February, reaching $27 billion. This increase was driven by a substantial rise in imports, especially gold and silver. Exports experienced a minor decrease. The government plans to introduce measures to support exporters facing logistical challenges. The trade gap with China also saw an increase during the fiscal year.

articles.title: India-US trade deal to be signed after new US tariff framework is finalised
articles.summary: India-US trade deal signing is contingent on a new tariff framework, with discussions ongoing regarding non-tariff barriers and Section 232 tariffs. The agreement will also address US Section 301 tariffs, with finalization awaiting Washington's restoration of global tariff architecture post-Supreme Court ruling.

articles.title: Revamped Stand-Up India scheme soon: Nirmala Sitharaman
articles.summary: Replying to a supplementary question in the Lok Sabha, Sitharaman said the modified programme would incorporate lessons from the earlier scheme, which was discontinued in April 2025 after nine years. "With studies done by Niti Aayog and others, this scheme is being redrafted and soon the Cabinet will be given a proposal with which we will launch a new scheme to continue with Stand-Up India, which benefitted SC/ST and women all over the country," she said.

articles.title: Govt approves additional 87,587 tonnes sugar export quota for 2025-26
articles.summary: In a significant move, India's food ministry has greenlit an additional quota of 87,587 tonnes of sugar exports for the upcoming 2025-26 season, responding to demands from sugar mills. These mills are required to complete the exports by June 30, 2026, with those meeting a threshold of 70 percent allowed to ship the remaining sugar by September 30, 2026.

articles.summary: India has initiated negotiations for trade agreements with the Philippines and the Maldives, signing terms of reference for a preferential trade agreement (PTA) with the former and a free trade agreement (FTA) with the latter. These moves come as India reviews its existing trade pacts with ASEAN and Korea, and pursues new agreements with several other nations and blocs.

articles.title: Shipping wars stitch higher costs into India’s apparel exports
articles.summary: India's apparel exporters are facing increased costs due to the West Asian conflict, with shipping routes extended and war surcharges adding ₹12 to ₹55 per garment. This disruption, including diversions around the Cape of Good Hope, delays deliveries by 10-15 days and impacts a key market for Indian ready-made garments.

articles.title: Unemployment rate eases to 4.9% in February
articles.summary: India's unemployment rate dipped to 4.9% in February, a slight improvement from January's 5%, largely due to a significant decrease in the female jobless rate. Urban unemployment also saw a decline, while rural rates remained stable. Youth unemployment, however, experienced a marginal increase.

articles.title: RBI wins Initiative of the Year award for domain rollout aimed at fighting cyberfraud
articles.summary: Launched in February 2025, the RBI has mandated banks to shift to a common ‘.bank.in’ domain that is restricted to regulated entities. The idea is to make it easier for users to identify authentic banking platforms and cut down on fraud linked to spoofed websites.

articles.title: Financial frauds cost global economy over $442 bn in 2025; risk in 2026 'high': Interpol
articles.summary: Interpol reported that financial frauds cost the global economy over $442 billion in 2025. The organization warned that the risk of such frauds remains high in 2026. The assessment underscores the need for stronger financial regulations and cybersecurity measures to combat fraudulent activities.
articles.raw_summary: Over USD 442 billion has been siphoned off from the global economy in 2025 through financial fraud, Interpol said in its global financial fraud threat assessment for 2026, released on Monday.

The deal could influence India's energy transition and trade dynamics, though it is a private sector initiative without direct government involvement.
